medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient with a history of chronic CHF (EF 30%) and recent diagnosis of afib on warfarin presented after fall at home. Reports feeling thirsty after having a cocktail and dinner but was not drinking other liquids. Stood up from chair, felt lightheaded, and fell to ground. No LOC. Found to have a C2 non-operative fracture. No chest pain, SOB, or palpitations.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Chronic Congestive Heart Failure LVEF 30%, Paget's disease, lumbar stenosis, BPH, colon cancer s/p resection many years ago, subarachnoid hemorrhage in the 1980s (spontaneous), c-spine surgery many years ago, s/p R hip and L knee replacements.
medical_history.family_history: No known family history of premature coronary artery disease, arrhythmia, or cardiomyopathy; otherwise noncontributory.

physical_examination.general_examination: VS 97.8 132/76 58 12 94%RA. Elderly gentleman, sitting in bed, neck brace in place in NAD. Small abrasion left side of forehead, EOMI, sclera anicteric. Bradycardic, normal s1,s2, no r/m/g appreciated. Bibasilar crackles up the lungs bilaterally, no wheezes or rhonchi. Abdomen soft, non-distended, non-tender to palpation, +BS. Bandage over left knee, warm/well perfused, 1+ radial and DP pulses.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Not specified. Cardiovascular: Bradycardic, normal s1,s2, no r/m/g appreciated. Other: Bibasilar crackles, bandage over left knee.
